steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the reference to the "wooing kestrel" in the text as a whole. Look at what the kestrel does in context: the speaker introduces it immediately after the friend describes how his soul feels calm from the lack of conflict. The speaker says the kestrel "mutes his mating-note to please the harmony of this sweet silence." The bird is silencing itself to match the same quiet the friend just described.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It suggests that there are some aspects of nature that will forever be mysterious to humans': nothing in the text suggests mystery or unknowability. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It emphasizes the speaker's belief that her friend is not sharing his true feelings with her': the opposite is true — the friend openly shares his feelings, and the speaker responds in kind. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It presents the natural world as aligning with the experience of the speaker and her friend': the kestrel actively joins the silence the friend described, showing nature mirroring their shared experience. ✓
  • Choice D — 'It illustrates the assertion that the definition of peace changes from person to person': the text never contrasts different definitions of peace; both characters share the same quiet. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
